mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the effective federal funds rate tightens monetary conditions, raising the discount rate on future corporate cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of month-over-month FEDFUNDS changes versus 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A threshold breach in which the unemployment rate increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals emerging labor market slack, reducing consumer income expectations and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the trigger occurs fewer than 10 times in a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in real retail sales and an increase in the unemployment rate interact to signal a consumer-led demand contraction; this joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the independent effects of either series.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS changes and UNRATE changes exhibits a Rank IC below 0.03 or the opposite sign over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a positive month-over-month change in the consumer price index pressures the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase the federal funds rate, compressing equity valuations through higher real discount rates and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3%, positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in the industrial production index signals rising corporate output and earnings capacity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in INDPRO show a Rank IC below 0.02 versus 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent over a three-month horizon signals contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances fail to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases over a 5-year rolling window, or the trigger fires fewer than 8 times in that window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production indicates coordinated expansion across labor and supply channels, amplifying earnings expectations and predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days beyond either variable alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS changes and INDPRO changes produces a negative Rank IC or magnitude below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarterly growth print in real chained GDP signals aggregate demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants re-price recession risk.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 prints are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 10-year rolling window.
